title: Staff Data Engineer ( AI & Analytics Data Products)

bulletPoints: Step into a pivotal role and become a key part in shaping our vision
Annual Performance Bonus Plan
AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology
content.jobHook: Join SEEK as a Staff Data Engineer to build high-quality data assets and enhance experiences for individuals and organizations.
content.unEditedContent:
Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

The Team

The AI & Analytics Data Products team builds high-quality data assets that empower teams across SEEK Group to gain valuable insights and enhance experiences for both individuals and organisations.

This position will report to the Engineering Manager and be part of a team made up of senior and mid-level data engineers. This role collaborates closely with internal peers, including staff, senior, and mid-level data engineers; analytics engineers; data solution designers; and data tracking specialists.

About the role 

The  Staff Data Engineer, is a senior technical leader within the AI & Analytics Data Products team, responsible for building high-quality data assets that empower teams across SEEK Group to deliver insights and enhance experiences for people and organisations.

As an in-team technical specialist, you design and build fit-for-purpose solutions while strengthening the team’s technical systems and platforms. You lead and elevate technical practices that enable effective software delivery, demonstrating deep expertise in the team’s codebase, systems, and tools.

You also drive alignment between your team’s technical roadmap and SEEK’s broader technology strategy.

Responsibilities 

  • Design, build and operate scalable, high‑performing data platforms and services aligned with SEEK’s technology strategy.
  • Ensure systems are well‑architected, secure, and meet SEEK’s technical and platform health metrics.
  • Lead and embed strong engineering and DevOps practices to continuously improve delivery and reliability.
  • Act as the technical escalation point, owning production issues through to resolution.
  • Champion clean, well‑tested, secure code and uplift technical capability within the team.
  • Collaborate effectively with Architecture, Security, and other technical stakeholders to align solutions.
  • Drive clear communication and alignment between technical work, team priorities, and customer outcomes.
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

  • Deep expertise in the Spark framework (Batch and Structured Streaming).
  • Proven experience building and operating large‑scale, cloud‑based data platforms and warehouses.
  • Strong proficiency with cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or Azure).
  • Advanced software engineering, coding, scripting, and SQL/database skills.
  • Skilled in logical and physical data modelling.
  • Hands‑on experience with DevOps practices — deployments, infrastructure, monitoring, and operations.
  • Demonstrated leadership in tech‑leading, mentoring, and developing engineers to enhance delivery performance.
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

  • Annual Performance Bonus Plan
  • Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
  • Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
  • Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
  • SEEK is committed to operating sustainably and is preparing for the impacts of climate change and the transition to a low-carbon future, and is working to minimise its environmental impacts which includes a long-term emissions reduction target of net zero by FY2050

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners

companyProfile.name: SEEK
companyProfile.overview: SEEK is a global leader in employment marketplaces, solving real problems for our customers and connecting the right work with the right people. Our platforms are used by approximately 50 million candidates worldwide, we have relationships with over 300,000 hirers and exposure to around 600 million people, which means we really know the markets we are operating in., We’re passionate about impacting people's lives when it matters most. We aspire to deliver excellence in everything we do and we continue to build a business that is an example to all.
companyProfile.perksAndBenefits: Free Breakfast, Support for parents, Community Impact, Learning & Development, Flexible Working, A commitment to operating sustainably, Leave benefits, Social events and casual dress

title: Senior AI Engineer

bulletPoints: Hybrid work enviornment
Onsite gym
High level of autonomy with your work
content.jobHook: We are seeking a Senior or Mid-level AI Engineer to advance our AI transformation.
content.unEditedContent:
About the Role

We are seeking a Senior or Mid-level AI Engineer to advance our AI transformation. In this role, you will bridge the gap between our existing Azure, .NET, and React-based enterprise systems with modern Azure AI capabilities, designing and delivering scalable AI solutions that provide measurable business value. You will work closely with technical teams and business stakeholders to implement intelligent solutions that enhance workflows and drive innovation across the organization.


Key Responsibilities

AI Strategy & Implementation: Lead the design and implementation of AI solutions using Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Foundry, and open-source LLMs.

Enterprise RAG Architecture: Architect and maintain production-grade Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines including data ingestion, embeddings, vector search, and response grounding.

System Integration: Develop and lead the integration of AI features into our existing Azure-based .NET platforms, ensuring seamless communication between AI models and legacy business logic.

Model Lifecycle & Operations (LLMOps): Evaluate, fine-tune, and optimize models; build or support CI/CD pipelines in Azure DevOps and monitor production systems.

Data, Performance & Cost Optimization: Partner with tech teams to design pipelines for high-quality model inputs and define best practices for prompt engineering, scalability, and cost efficiency.

Governance & Responsible AI: Implement Responsible AI guardrails, including model monitoring, security compliance, and ethical AI standards.

Leadership & Mentorship: Act as a technical authority, mentoring engineers and partnering with cross-functional stakeholders to identify and prioritize high-impact AI use cases.

Documentation: Document, maintain, and promote reusable AI components and patterns for internal adoption.


Required Skills & Experience

Software Engineering: 8+ years of IT industry experience with .NET and Azure, demonstrating strong skills in system design, API development, data processing, and system integration.

Azure AI & Platform Expertise: 3+ Hands-on experience with Azure OpenAI, Azure Machine Learning, Azure AI Search, and Databricks, delivering scalable, production-grade AI and ML solutions.

LLM & RAG Systems: Proven success designing and deploying LLM-powered applications, including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures, embeddings, vector search, and response grounding.

AI & ML Foundations: Deep understanding of prompt engineering, inference optimization, and vector databases such as Azure AI Search, Pinecone, and Weaviate.

Collaboration & Governance: Strong ability to collaborate across tech, and business teams, with working knowledge of data governance, security, and Responsible AI practices.

Generative & Agentic AI: Demonstrated experience building Generative and Agentic AI solutions using platforms such as OpenAI, Hugging Face, or equivalent technologies.

If you are looking for a growing and rewarding career in IT, please submit your resume and cover letter for consideration. Applications submitted without a resume and cover letter will not be considered. We look forward to hearing from you!

Please be aware that you must have the right to live and work in Australia.

employerQuestions: Which of the following statements best describes your right to work in Australia?
How many years' experience do you have as an Artificial Intelligence Engineer?

title: Software Engineer – AI & Data (Platform Growth)

bulletPoints: Real AI. Real Impact.
Fast-Track Platform Ownership
High Responsibility, High Growth
content.jobHook: Build real-world AI systems at national scale and fast-track your path to core platform ownership in a high-growth tech company.
content.unEditedContent:
Full-time | Melbourne (Hybrid)

https://www.realtimetraffic.com.au/

Real Time Traffic (RTT) is Australia's leading AI and computer vision company for road safety. We work with transport agencies across multiple states to deploy AI-powered camera systems that detect near-misses, identify high-risk sites, and support proactive decision-making that saves lives.

Our technology is live at hundreds of locations across Australia, with thousands more planned. We're in a high-growth phase with a roadmap to expand internationally.

As demand accelerates, we're scaling fast and we're looking for a high-calibre engineer who wants real responsibility early.


The Mission of This Role


Our engineering team is heavily involved in day-to-day delivery execution. That model doesn't scale, and it doesn't allow us to evolve the platform at the pace the market demands.

We need someone exceptional who can:

  • Absorb delivery complexity

  • Engineer scalable interim solutions

  • Reduce operational drag

  • Identify repeat patterns in client needs

  • Convert real-world friction into validated product improvements

This role starts close to delivery but it is designed to evolve into core platform ownership. We are deliberately building this role as a bridge between real-world deployment complexity and long-term platform design.


Why This Role Is Exciting


This role gives you:

  • Exposure to live AI systems operating at national scale

  • Direct visibility of how product decisions affect real deployments

  • Responsibility to design pragmatic, production-grade solutions

  • A pathway into core platform architecture and roadmap input

If you perform and choose to, you will progressively transition from delivery-focused engineering into proactive system development and platform evolution.

We are intentionally hiring for someone who wants that trajectory.


What You'll Be Doing


1. Engineer Creative Scalable Solutions Under Real Constraints

  • Work directly with the delivery team to solve technical bottlenecks

  • Extend existing systems intelligently (not hack around them)

  • Design reusable tooling and automation to eliminate recurring manual work

  • Improve quality, reliability, and turnaround time across deployments

2. Turn Delivery Friction into Product Insight

  • Identify patterns in client requirements that exceed current platform capabilities

  • Validate whether these represent scalable product opportunities

  • Contribute structured thinking into roadmap discussions

  • Help us decide what becomes permanent product vs. what remains bespoke)

3. Work with Real Data & Applied AI

  • Analyse traffic, behavioural, and video-derived datasets

  • Build Python-based processing and validation tools

  • Contribute to LLM-driven reporting and automation workflows

  • Support refinement of AI-enabled operational systems

  • Improve how we operationalise machine learning outputs


Tech Environment


  • Languages: Python (pandas, numpy), JavaScript

  • Cloud: AWS (Lambda, S3, Kinesis, EMR)

  • Databases: MongoDB

  • Tools: Git, Jupyter, Docker, Linux, CI/CD tools

  • Other Technologies in Use: OpenCV for computer vision, LLM-based automation tools, ISO 27001 security frameworks

You must be genuinely excited about applying AI to solve real operational problems.


Our Values


  • Compassion – We build technology that protects lives. We care about our clients, our communities, and each other. Impact matters and so do people.

  • Accountability – We take ownership of outcomes, not just tasks. We hold ourselves to high standards and stand behind our work.

  • Responsiveness – We move with urgency and clarity. Whether supporting clients or teammates, we act decisively and communicate openly.

  • Excellence – We pursue technical rigour and continuous improvement. We challenge assumptions, refine systems, and raise the bar.

  • Safety – Safety is not a feature; it's our purpose. From secure systems to safer roads, we engineer responsibly and think long term.


About You


This role is for someone who:

  • Has 2 years of strong engineering experience

  • Thinks in systems, not tickets

  • Enjoys ambiguity and solving messy real-world problems

  • Can balance pragmatism with long-term thinking

  • Proactively takes ownership and closes loops without supervision

  • Communicates clearly and directly

Required

  • Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, or similar

  • Intermediate level Python development experience with demonstrated ability to build data processing and validation tools

  • Experience working with data-heavy systems

  • Comfortable working in cloud environments

  • Melbourne-based (or relocating)

  • Must have current Australian work rights at the time of application

Strongly Valued

  • Exposure to AI/ML workflows

  • Experience building internal tooling or automation

  • Experience supporting operational or delivery teams

  • High intellectual curiosity


What Success Looks Like (12–18 Months)


  • Delivery reliance on core engineers significantly reduced

  • Recurring manual processes automated

  • Interim solutions structured and reusable

  • Clear product opportunities identified and validated

  • Active contribution to core platform evolution

  • Participation in architectural discussions

If you're exceptional, this role evolves into:

  • Core platform engineer

  • Technical lead on scalable modules

  • Strategic contributor to RTT's long-term product direction


How to Apply

Please submit your CV and a brief introduction via Seek.
Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Apply early to be considered.

This Role Is Not For:

  • Engineers who prefer clearly scoped tickets

  • Those uncomfortable with ambiguity

  • People who want to stay narrowly specialised

This role is for someone who wants to grow fast, take ownership early, and help build a category leader.

employerQuestions: Which of the following statements best describes your right to work in Australia?
How many years' experience do you have as an Artificial Intelligence Engineer?
Have you completed a qualification in computer science?

bulletPoints: 6 Month contract || Hybrid role
Rowville, Melbourne || $900-$1000 including super
Must- Modern AI, LLM, Agentic AI, Gen AI, Cloud- Azure or GCP, Data Science/ML
content.jobHook: Exciting opportunity for AI Engineer with our Automotive client in Melbourne on contract basis.
content.unEditedContent:
About the Role :

We are seeking an experienced AI Engineer with strong expertise in Large Language Models (LLMs), Generative AI, and Agentic AI systems to join our Melbourne-based team on a 6-month contract.
This role will focus on designing, developing, and deploying AI-powered solutions leveraging modern ML frameworks and cloud platforms (Azure or GCP). 
Key Responsibilities
  • Design and develop AI solutions using LLMs and Generative AI models
  • Build and implement Agentic AI systems (multi-agent workflows, tool use, orchestration frameworks)
  • Develop ML pipelines from data preprocessing to model deployment
  • Fine-tune and optimize foundation models for business use cases
  • Implement RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architectures
  • Work with structured and unstructured datasets
  • Collaborate with data engineers and cloud teams for scalable deployment
  • Deploy AI solutions using Azure (OpenAI, ML Studio, Cognitive Services) or GCP (Vertex AI, BigQuery, AI Platform)
  • Ensure model performance, scalability, and security
  • Conduct experimentation, A/B testing, and statistical validation
  • Document technical solutions and best practices
Required Skills & Experience
  • 4+ years experience in AI/ML or Data Science roles
  • Strong hands-on experience with:
    • Large Language Models (LLMs)
    • Generative AI frameworks
    • Agentic AI / AI orchestration frameworks
  • Strong foundation in:
    • Statistics & Probability
    • Linear Algebra
    • Calculus
    • Optimization techniques
  • Experience with cloud platforms:
    • Microsoft Azure OR
    • Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
  • Excellent communication skills
If matches then apply through ad.

bulletPoints: Design and deploy enterprise-grade Generative AI solutions
Work across LLMs, RAG architectures and full-stack AI platforms
Shape scalable AI platforms used at scale
content.jobHook: AI Engineer delivering enterprise-grade Generative AI solutions across LLM, RAG and full-stack AI platforms.
content.unEditedContent:
AI Engineer

Sydney / Melbourne | Contract

Tenth Revolution Group is partnering with a global technology consultancy to appoint a AI Engineer specialising in Generative AI and enterprise AI platforms.

This is a senior technical consulting role suited to someone who combines deep hands-on engineering capability with strong client engagement skills. You'll design and deliver production-grade GenAI systems while helping shape AI capability across large enterprise environments.

The Opportunity

You'll operate at the intersection of advanced AI engineering and client advisory, delivering scalable, secure, enterprise-ready AI solutions.

This role is ideal for someone who:

  • Has deployed Generative AI into production environments
  • Enjoys solving complex enterprise challenges
  • Can translate emerging AI capabilities into real business outcomes
  • Is comfortable working directly with senior stakeholders

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and implement production-grade GenAI solutions
  • Build and deploy systems leveraging LLMs, embeddings, and retrieval-based architectures
  • Develop secure, scalable AI platforms integrating data pipelines and APIs
  • Deliver full-stack AI solutions, including backend services and user-facing applications
  • Ensure enterprise standards across performance, security, compliance and maintainability

Skills & Experience

  • Strong software engineering background with hands-on AI/ML delivery
  • Recent experience deploying Generative AI solutions into production

  • Experience working with L

    arge Language Models (LLMs), embedding and vector search, retrieval-augmented architectures, Prompt engineering and orchestration frameworks
  • Proficiency in Python and modern backend development
  • Experience with modern frontend frameworks (React, Angular or similar)
  • Strong cloud experience deploying AI workloads in enterprise environments
  • Understanding of MLOps / DevOps practices for scaling AI systems
  • Ability to communicate complex AI concepts to technical and business stakeholders
  • Consulting or client-facing experience highly regarded

Why Apply?

  • Work on enterprise-scale AI transformation programs
  • Influence how Generative AI is deployed across major organisations
  • Combine deep technical work with advisory exposure
  • Join a growing AI capability within a global consulting environment

Apply now or contact Tenth Revolution Group for a confidential discussion.

Contact:

Neros Gorges
(03) 8592 0507
n.gorges@tenthrevolution.com

#SCR-neros-gorges

bulletPoints: Join a boutique, expert-led consulting team at Davidson Technology Consulting,
Design, develop, and implement AI-driven solutions
Contract role - ASAP start
content.jobHook: 🚀 EOI Wanted - AI Engineer (GenAI/RAG) | 6-month contract | Melbourne CBD | Hybrid 2-3 days onsite ☁️🤖 late Feb / Early March 2026 start.
content.unEditedContent:
📍 Melbourne CBD

💼 Full-time Contract, Initial 6 months

🤖 AI | 📊 Data | ☁️ Cloud

Hybrid working: 2 days on-site



We are looking for an AI Engineer to design, develop, and implement AI-driven solutions, supporting high-impact AI project in the utilities, financial services, manufacturing and mining sectors. This role will focus on developing GenAI models, optimising vector databases, and advancing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) workflows.



Role Overview

· Build and deploy production-ready AI/ML pipelines.

· Train and fine-tune LLMs and Generative AI models for enterprise applications.

· Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and vector database optimisations.

· Work with Python, Hugging Face, OpenAI APIs, LangChain, and related AI/ML frameworks.

· Leverage AWS SageMaker, Azure ML/OpenAI, and GCP Vertex AI for cloud-based AI solutions.

· Collaborate with data scientists, engineers, and product teams to integrate AI into real-world systems.



About You

· Experience with transformers, diffusion models, and LLMs (GPT, BERT, Stable Diffusion, etc.).

· Proficiency in Python, NumPy, Pandas, and AI/ML libraries.

· Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, OpenSearch) and advanced RAG workflows.

· Skilled in AI/ML pipeline development, data engineering, and cloud-based AI deployments.

· Passionate about emerging AI advancements, tools and frameworks.

· Current / previous experience in utilities and data migration would be a plus



Key Requirements

· Experienced in building production-ready AI/ML solutions.

· Experience working with Generative AI, RAG, and prompt engineering.

· Experience in vector databases, AI model training, and scalable AI infrastructure.

· Familiarity with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and other GenAI technology stacks.

· Strong skills in Git, DevOps, and AI model versioning.



About us




Davidson Technology Consulting is a boutique, expert-driven business and technology consultancy on a mission to help organisations drive lasting change. We empower clients to build high-performing functions while leveraging our expertise to lead transformation and innovation.



We guide clients from strategy to execution, scaling delivery and embedding advanced technology to accelerate change and build resilient, future-ready organisations.



How to Apply

If you would like to have a confidential discussion, please contact Amanda Aing via amanda.aing@davidsonwp.com, quoting reference JN -022026-43442.




Want to know more about Davidson? Visit us at www.davidsonwp.com

title: AI Engineer

content.jobHook: Build and operate production-grade AI infrastructure that designers trust and the business depends on.
content.unEditedContent:
The Company

Harry The Hirer is Australia’s leading events company. For 40+ years, we’ve powered the country’s most iconic events: Australian Open, Formula 1 Grand Prix, MotoGP, Melbourne Cup.


The Role

Build and operate production-grade AI infrastructure that designers trust and the business relies on daily. You’ll own ML deployment, monitoring, reliability, and cost optimisation from Day 1. You’ll join a small, focused team with direct ownership and accountability. You’ll inherit a working AI visualisation platform already in production and take ownership of hardening and scaling it.


What you'll actually build

→ Multi-step agentic workflows using LLM tool use and structured reasoning, built directly against model APIs, not framework-dependent

→ API orchestration across LLM, vision, and generative models with fallbacks, retries, and cost controls

→ Design and implement measurable evaluation layers for generative systems, including correctness, constraint validation, regression detection, and quality gates before outputs reach users

→ Observability and tracing for agent pipelines at the prompt and chain level

→ Prompt and pipeline versioning, caching strategies, and token cost management

→ Reliable deployment patterns that the team can ship against without bottlenecks


Current stack: direct model APIs, serverless backend, structured data pipelines, production logging and tracing. This is applied infrastructure, not research. You will work with real business constraints, real cost ceilings, and real users.


Success metrics:

→ 99 percent+ successful job completion across agent workflows with monitored failure recovery

→ Cost per generation decreases over time

→ Zero silent failures in production

→ Other engineers deploy independently using your documentation

→ Measurable workflow time savings within 3 to 6 months


Must Have

→ Built AI systems using LLM APIs and agentic patterns (tool use, multi-step reasoning, structured outputs)

→ Shipped generative AI features that real users depend on

→ Experience with API cost optimisation, rate limiting, and reliability

→ Comfortable with resource constraints and explicit success criteria

→ Documentation-first approach

→ Experience designing monitoring and alerting for ML or LLM systems (logs, traces, cost metrics, failure detection)

→ Strong software engineering fundamentals (Python or TypeScript), version control, testing discipline

→ Experience working directly with model APIs rather than relying solely on orchestration frameworks


Nice to Have

→ Experience building against Anthropic (Claude) or OpenAI APIs directly, not just through wrapper frameworks

→ AI work in non-tech industries

→ Serverless architectures (Vercel, Supabase) and/or cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP/Azure)

→ Built systems under strict scope control and conservative rollout cadences


About You

You ship production AI systems, not architecture decks. You've wired together LLMs, vision models, and business logic into things people actually use. You think in agentic patterns, not framework lock-in. You're energised by greenfield infrastructure with real business constraints.


Reality check: We are an established company, not a startup. Decisions move through clear ownership, and new systems are introduced deliberately. We value disciplined execution, measured rollouts, and long-term reliability over rapid experimentation.


The Details

Reporting to: AI Systems Lead

Location: Melbourne (Abbotsford, on-site)

Start: ASAP

Apply: [simonel@harrythehirer.com.au]


No Agencies Please.


Include in the email Subject : “AI Engineering ”

Include your resume and answer:

Describe a production ML or LLM system you built and operated.

How did you monitor it?

What metrics did you track?

What failed in production, and how did you detect and resolve it?


Harry The Hirer

Australian Open | Formula 1 | MotoGP | Melbourne Cup

www.harrythehirer.com.au

employerQuestions: Which of the following statements best describes your right to work in Australia?

bulletPoints: Lead AI adoption across People & Culture
High-impact role in a growing organisation
Hybrid role
content.jobHook: An exciting opportunity to bridge engineering, AI, and product decisions!
content.unEditedContent:
Company description:

Accelerate AI adoption. Influence how we hire.

Established in 1840, we're Australia's first member-owned wellbeing company. Today we have over $1 billion in revenue and provide smart solutions and services to more than 700,000 Australians. We employ over 7000 people and our purpose is to Help People to Thrive.

It's an exciting time to be joining Australian Unity - we have grown significantly over recent years and are transforming to capitalise on further growth opportunities to help our customers and employees thrive. We operate with commercial principles and with a strong social purpose to create community value. Australian Unity is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.



Job description:

You will lead the end‑to‑end adoption of AI solutions within our People & Culture function - from identifying opportunities to delivering real business value. A major early focus will be partnering with our recruitment platform vendor to ensure their AI models meet our standards for fairness, transparency, performance, and compliance.

In this role, you will:

  • Lead end‑to‑end AI adoption within a business squad - from identifying opportunities and shaping requirements to solution selection, delivery, and post‑launch support.
  • Work directly with AI vendors - challenging assumptions, asking the right technical questions, evaluating model behaviour, ensuring fairness and performance, and influencing product direction.
  • Partner with internal technical and business stakeholders to shape use cases, drive adoption, build user confidence, and ensure AI solutions deliver measurable business value.
  • Ensure all AI initiatives meet governance, risk, and compliance expectations, and that solutions are well‑designed, documented, and aligned with enterprise standards.
  • Contribute to the evolution of enterprise‑level AI practices in an area such as AI literacy & change, AI governance, or AIOps / AI innovation.

You'll be part of a small, expert group helping Australian Unity become an AI‑native enterprise - and you'll have the freedom to shape how we get there.

About You:

We're looking for someone with a strong technical foundation who is ready to apply it in a more product‑oriented role. You may come from a software engineering or computer science background with hands‑on AI experience, and you've either already made the move into product-style work - or you're excited to take that next step.

You enjoy solving business problems, engaging with stakeholders, and challenging vendors with the right technical questions. You're as comfortable discussing model behaviour and risks as you are explaining concepts to non‑technical audiences.

To be successful in the role you will be able to demonstrate the following capabilities / experience:

  • Hands‑on industry experience (3+ years) in software engineering, AI engineering, data science, or computer science
  • Practical experience applying AI/ML/LLM technologies in real-world projects
  • Experience translating business needs into technical or product requirements
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify complex AI concepts for non‑technical audiences
  • Experience delivering solutions in regulated or risk-aware environments (privacy, fairness, model behaviour) is a plus
  • Exposure to AI governance, AI literacy/change, AIOps/MLOps/LLMOps, model evaluation, or data privacy considerations (desirable)
  • Hands-on experience with low-code and automation platforms e.g., Microsoft Power Automate, Copilot Studio (desirable)

What's on Offer:

You will enjoy a range of great employee benefits and rewards including:

  • Competitive salary + bonus program
  • Enjoy additional yearly Well-Being and Community leave days
  • 14-week paid parental leave, with equal benefit for both parents
  • Employee Referral Program
  • Employee Assistance Program - support and assistance for you and your partner at those times in life when you need it most
  • Maxxia Rewards - a great range of discounts and benefits at selected retail outlets, department stores, attractions, travel, cinemas, restaurants.
  • Discounts across the Australian Unity business including Private Health Insurance, General Insurance + more!
  • Available access to LinkedIn Learning courses through our great Learning platform

For further benefits visit: https://www.australianunity.com.au/careers/culture

Australian Unity is an Equal Opportunity employer and we encourage applications from all members of the community, including people of Aboriginal and Torres strait Islander descent, culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds and, mature aged people.

companyProfile.name: Australian Unity
companyProfile.overview: Established in 1840, we’re Australia’s first member-owned wellbeing company, delivering health, wealth and living services. We’re committed to real wellbeing for all Australians, and for us, real wellbeing means so much more than physical health. It’s about your standard of living and feeling safe in your home. It’s your personal relationships and being connected to your community. It’s about what you want to achieve in life, while having the security to get out and do what makes you happy.

bulletPoints: Attractive package with great benefits & career growth opportunities
Melbourne CBD / Hybrid Work / 2 - 3 Days on Site
Opportunity to work with cutting-edge ML, LLMs, and GenAI
content.jobHook: AI & Innovation Engineer responsible for designing, developing, and implementing comprehensive test automation strategies for an AI-powered system
content.unEditedContent:
We are seeking an experienced AI & Innovation Engineer to join our Technology Delivery team supporting a strategic AI-powered system.

This role plays a critical part in ensuring the quality, reliability, and performance of modern Machine Learning, Large Language Model (LLM), and Generative AI solutions. You will design and implement scalable automation frameworks across the full technology stack - from React-based user interfaces through to backend services, AI models, search platforms, and data orchestration pipelines.

You will lead testing innovation for AI-driven applications, validating contextual accuracy, factual correctness, and ongoing performance of LLM models while enabling continuous delivery of platform enhancements.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and implement enterprise-grade test automation frameworks across UI, API, backend, and AI services

  • Develop automated testing strategies for ML, LLM, and Generative AI applications

  • Validate AI outputs for accuracy, relevance, and response quality across multiple models

  • Integrate automation into CI/CD pipelines to support continuous delivery

  • Build end-to-end regression and performance test suites

  • Monitor automation metrics and provide actionable quality insights

  • Lead test planning, coverage strategy, and risk-based testing activities

  • Mentor junior engineers and promote automation best practices

  • Collaborate closely with developers, product teams, and stakeholders

  • Drive continuous improvement of testing frameworks and DevOps practices

Technical Environment

  • Languages & Tools: Python, pytest, FastAPI

  • Automation: Playwright, Selenium, Appium, REST Assured

  • AI & Data: Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Databricks, Elasticsearch, Vector Search

  • Frontend & Backend: React / Next.js, APIs, distributed services

  • Cloud & DevOps: AWS, Azure, Git, CI/CD pipelines

Skills & Experience

  • Strong experience designing scalable test automation frameworks

  • Hands-on testing of AI, NLP, or LLM-based applications

  • Advanced Python automation development

  • Experience testing complex distributed systems, APIs, and data platforms

  • Deep understanding of Agile delivery and DevOps practices

  • Experience with cloud-based testing environments

  • Ability to produce meaningful quality metrics and reporting

What You'll Bring

  • Technical leadership in automation and quality engineering

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving capability

  • Passion for emerging AI technologies and innovation

  • Ability to work autonomously while contributing to high-performing teams

Desirable Certifications

  • ISTQB Advanced Test Automation Engineering (CTAL-TAE)

  • AI/ML or Data Science certifications

  • Python or SQL certifications

Why Join Us?

This is an opportunity to work at the forefront of AI and Generative AI technology, shaping testing practices for next-generation intelligent platforms while delivering meaningful enterprise impact.

For more information or a confidential discussion about this role, please contact Ronald at ronald.tran@talentinternational.com

advertiser.name: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond

title: Lead AI Engineer - Gain Production GenAI/Agentic AI -$220,000-Melbourne/Hybrid

content.jobHook: Lead AI Engineer - Gain Production GenAI/Agentic AI - $220,000 - Melbourne - Hybrid    Morgan Consulting is partnering with a data driven
content.unEditedContent:
Lead AI Engineer - Gain Production GenAI/Agentic AI - $220,000 - Melbourne - Hybrid 

Morgan Consulting is partnering with a data driven innovator that is shaping the next era of Generative AI, Agentic AI, and Machine Learning. As they scale advanced AI capabilities across products, platforms, and engineering practices, they are seeking a Lead AI Engineer to define technical direction, build high impact solutions, and mentor a cross‑functional squad working across production GenAI, Agentic AI and ML Systems.


About the Role:(reference V-66350)

This is a hands‑on technical leadership role, you will architect, build, and operate reliable ML/AI solutions, elevate engineering standards, and accelerate the adoption of Generative and Agentic AI across mission‑critical, client‑facing platforms. This position combines strategic influence with deep technical execution, ideal for someone who wants to lead, innovate, and deliver tangible impact.

What You Will Do:
  • Set technical vision and roadmap for ML/AI engineering, including GenAI testing, automation, agentic patterns, MLOps, and production GenAI.
  • Lead and mentor engineers across automation, frontend testing, and ML through reviews, pairing, and capability uplift.
  • Architect end‑to‑end ML/AI systems: feature pipelines, model training/evaluation, inference/agentic services, observability, and scalable continuous delivery.
  • Establish GenAI and Agentic AI testing frameworks, including custom LLM test models, and drive best practice in evaluation, safety, and release governance.
  • Build and evolve automation frameworks for web/mobile (Playwright, Cypress, Appium/Detox) with GenAI‑driven test generation, prioritisation, and self‑healing.
  • Standardise ML/GenAI delivery patterns: feature stores, CI/CD, registries, canary/blue‑green, rollbacks, A/B & back‑testing, agent orchestration.
  • Own reliability, performance, resilience, latency, and cost optimisation; ensure SLOs, alerting, and runbooks for ML and production GenAI.
  • Close gaps across DevSecOps, guardrails, data governance, secrets, PII handling, and compliance for ML, LLMs, and agents.
  • Partner with Data Science to productionise advanced models (LLMs, multimodal, agentic systems) with experimentation, telemetry, and monitoring.
  • Champion continuous improvement, automation‑first mindset, developer experience uplift, and platform investments.
  • Design and implement feature pipelines, inference services, and agentic execution paths (APIs, streaming, batch) with drift, hallucination, performance, and cost monitoring.
  • Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for ML, GenAI, and automation (GitHub Actions primary; Jenkins/Codemagic as needed).
  • Lead test strategy for ML, LLMs, and agentic systems: contract tests, model evals, guardrails, synthetic data, behavioural tests, production validation.
  • Perform peer reviews and ensure engineering standards: secure coding, documentation, reproducibility.
  • Drive estimation, prioritisation, and delivery alignment with product and platform teams.
  • Diagnose and resolve production issues across ML/LLM failures, agentic misbehaviour, data drift, and infra bottlenecks; own incident analysis and remediation.
  • Ensure observability and explainability: metrics, logs, traces, model cards, evaluation reports for all ML/AI systems.
  • Support adoption of quantitative/price‑assurance frameworks and contribute to solver/simulation‑based applications.
What you will bring:

Proven experience leading engineering teams or guilds across Gen AI and Agentic AI/ML/MLOps and test automation in production, customer-facing contexts.
Strong software engineering in Python and one of Rust/C++/Java/Go, with demonstrable knowledge of concurrency, parallelisation, performance tuning, and memory safety.
Hands-on with ML pipelines, model registries, feature stores, containerisation (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes).
Expertise in CI/CD and quality engineering at scale; deep experience integrating GitHub Actions (preferred) or Jenkins/Codemagic.
Solid track record with frontend/mobile test frameworks (Playwright, Cypress, Appium/Detox, Selenium) and GenAI enabled testing (test synthesis, self healing, flake reduction).
Proficient in model experimentation & evaluation (A/B testing, back testing, cross validation, error analysis), plus real time prediction use cases (regression, classification, time-series, anomaly detection).
Strong grasp of DevSecOps for ML, secrets, compliance, access control, PII handling, dependency risk, and secure SDLC.
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and the ability to influence standards across multiple teams.
Experience with quant/price assurance frameworks or solver/simulation based applications.
Familiarity with data models such as Medallion, Kimball, or Data Vault.
TypeScript/JavaScript for tooling and test frameworks; mobile CI familiarity.
Knowledge of LLM evaluation frameworks, prompt testing/hardening, and guardrail libraries.

Why Join:

Opportunity to gain significant GenAI and Agentic AI production experience at enterprise scale. 
Strong Career Development.
Amazing culture - data driven innovator that is shaping the next era of Generative AI, Agentic AI, and Machine Learning.

If you're a Lead AI Engineer, who thrives in a fast-paced environment, and is outcome focussed we would love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out for a confidential conversation (reference V-66350).
